steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a verb form question. “Yet” is a coordinating conjunction joining two independent clauses: “We came to see the famous artworks” and “the building itself ______ us the most.” The blank must be a finite past-tense verb to complete the second independent clause and match the past-tense verb “came” in the first clause.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — impressed: Simple past tense — a finite verb that can serve as the main verb of an independent clause and agrees in tense with “came.” ✓
  • Choice B — impressing: Present participle — a non-finite form that cannot stand alone as a main verb; using it creates a clause fragment with no finite verb. ✗
  • Choice C — having impressed: Perfect participle — another non-finite form; it cannot function as a main verb and would turn the second clause into a participial phrase rather than an independent clause. ✗
  • Choice D — to impress: Infinitive — a non-finite form that expresses purpose or intent, not a completed action; it cannot serve as the main verb of an independent clause. ✗
That matches Choice A — impressed.
